Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Decedents Estate
Off Calendar Jan-21-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Jaynes appeared by zoom for and with Andrew Lorek by zoom.
Patrick Simon (decedent's father) appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Jayanthi Simon (decedent's mother) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Ms. Jaynes spoke of ownership percentages in the property between the decedent and her parents, stated that the way title of the property was taken was kind of faulty on its face as joint tenants, that it shows incorrect percentages and that the parties intended to take the property in tenants in common. The court stated that by looking at this particular title it can't tell if it's faulty and that the property could be held that way. Ms. Jaynes agrees that a lay person looking at it would not know it's faulty. Discussion was held regarding the issue of Mr. Lorek providing timely notice as guardian of Leander and Georgina prior to him being appointed. Ms. Jaynes stated that it was 28-days as opposed to 30-days when calculating it, that they waited for the supplement to be filed before they mailed it, that the guardianship hearing is today and that they re-noticed everything. The court waives the issue of timing of the notice, informed Ms. Jaynes that a supplement does not amend the prayer and that the supplement asked for more relief. Ms. Jaynes stated that the original petition/order was going to distribute the property as a 70% interest to the decedent's estate and a 30% interest to the parents as tenants in common, that the only thing they didn't specify in the original petition was the word "reformation", that she thinks the petition and the supplement are asking for the same relief, and that she provided a new order that states reformation. The court stated that reformation is a critical term in proper litigation, that reformation has its own process and is quite involved if parties are in a civil department, raised the issue of whether this is appropriate, whether this is a quiet title or an 850 petition, and stated that quiet title actions have a lot of requirements that go beyond the folks who are on the title. Ms. Jaynes stated that everyone is here, that everyone agrees to the petition, that the property is not rented, that there are no other interested parties and that she believes it is properly before the court. Ms. Jaynes also stated that they are seeking that 70% of the property go to the estate. Mr. and Mrs. Simon both stated that they are consenting to the relief that is being requested in the supplement. The court questions why there is no inventory and appraisal. Ms. Jaynes stated that she needs to know which property is in the estate and that she will prepare it as soon as she gets this order. The Petition to Establish Estate's Claim of Ownership to Real Property, etc. is under submission. Ms. Jaynes is to monitor the court's register of actions/website to provide timely notice of the order. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for March 1, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the inventory and appraisal is not filed by February 22, 2022, Ms. Jaynes needs to file something explaining why.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Decedents Estate
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Continued To Jan-04-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Jaynes, via ZOOM, for Andrew Lorek, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

The court begins by querying Ms. Jaynes re whether she has seen the notes in this matter from the assigned Examiner. Ms. Jaynes reports that she has and points to the notice issue and confirms that both minors in this matter are under twelve (12) years old and asks the court to take her testimony in to consideration in lieu of her needing to file any additional documentation re the preceding. The court agrees and moves to the issue of bond. Ms. Jaynes reports that she intends to file a guardianship petition soon on behalf of her client and that her client has been approved for the bond amount being suggested by the court.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Letters of Administration is submitted. Bond amount is to be determined by the court.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.
